report_name: Hardcoded ClickUp API Key Exposes 959 Email Addresses from Fortune 500 Firms and Government Agencies

attack_patterns.name: Unsecured Credentials
attack_patterns.description: A hardcoded API key for the Split.io service was discovered in a public JavaScript file on ClickUp's website, allowing unauthenticated access to sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: An unauthenticated attacker could exploit a hardcoded API key found in a public JavaScript file on ClickUp's website to make unauthorized API calls to the third-party service Split.io and download sensitive data.

vulnerabilities.name: Hardcoded API Key in Public JavaScript File
vulnerabilities.description: A static API key for the Split.io service was hardcoded into a public JavaScript file on ClickUp's website, enabling unauthenticated access to an internal data file containing email addresses and feature flags.

identities.name: ClickUp
identities.description: The project management platform whose public website contained a hardcoded API key, leading to a data leak.

identities.name: Split.io
identities.description: A third-party feature management platform whose API was accessed using the leaked key from ClickUp.

identities.name: Home Depot
identities.description: One of the corporations whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Autodesk
identities.description: One of the corporations whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Rakuten
identities.description: One of the corporations whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Fortinet
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Tenable
identities.description: A cybersecurity firm whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Mayo Clinic
identities.description: A healthcare provider whose employee email addresses were exposed in the ClickUp data leak.

identities.name: Government Agencies
identities.description: Government workers in the U.S. and Australia were among those whose email addresses were exposed in the leak.

infrastructure.name: ClickUp Public Website
infrastructure.description: The public-facing website that hosted the JavaScript file containing the hardcoded API key.

infrastructure.name: Split.io API
infrastructure.description: The third-party API that was queried by an attacker using the leaked key to download a file of email addresses and feature flags.
